gloss:
If an encoder maps two inputs with different target values to the same representation, then no predictor built on that representation can recover the target. The JEPA collapse failure is a direct consequence of the identification boundary, not a training artifact.

**Cites** `TLT.NonIdentifiability.JEPA.jepa_target_not_identifiable` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The raw lemma is a non-identifiability fact about encoders; the thesis-layer statement recasts a well-known representation-learning failure mode as an instance of the general refinement clause, not a pathology of one method.

statement.pretty: ∀ {Input Context Target : Type} (E : Input → Context) (T : Input → Target) {i₁ i₂ : Input}, E i₁ = E i₂ → T i₁ ≠ T i₂ → ¬∃ g, ∀ (x : Input), T x = g (E x)

title: Eidometry.Thesis.learning_boundary
